summary: Azure provides comprehensive threat protection through services such as Microsoft Defender for Cloud, Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Entra ID Protection, and Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps. This collection of security services and capabilities offers advanced detection, response, and threat intelligence to protect your Azure deployments.
classification_reason: Overview of Azure threat protection services (Defender for Cloud, Sentinel, etc.); primarily descriptive without detailed error codes, configs, or limits.

title: Trusted Hardware Identity Management
description: Technical overview of Trusted Hardware Identity Management, which handles cache management of certificates and provides a trusted computing base.
summary: The Trusted Hardware Identity Management service handles cache management of certificates for all trusted execution environments (TEEs) that reside in Azure. It also provides trusted computing base (TCB) information to enforce a minimum baseline for attestation solutions.
classification_reason: Technical overview of Trusted Hardware Identity Management and its role; appears conceptual without exposed configuration parameters, limits, or troubleshooting mappings.

title: Security features used with Azure VMs - Azure security
description: This article provides an overview of the core Azure security features that can be used with Azure Virtual Machines.
summary: This article provides an overview of core Azure security features for virtual machines. Azure Virtual Machines lets you deploy a wide range of computing solutions in an agile way. The service supports Microsoft Windows, Linux, Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, IBM, SAP, and Azure BizTalk Services. You can deploy any workload and any language on nearly any operating system. title: Zero Trust security in Azure
description: Learn how to apply the guiding principles of Zero Trust to Azure infrastructure and services.
summary: Zero Trust is a security strategy that assumes breach and verifies each request as though it originated from an uncontrolled network. This article introduces Zero Trust principles and how they apply to Microsoft Azure. Tip For comprehensive Zero Trust guidance, see theZero Trust documentationwhich includes: This article provides an Azure-focused introduction to Zero Trust concepts.
classification_reason: Introduces Zero Trust principles in Azure at a conceptual level; no detailed RBAC role lists, config parameters, or product-specific settings. Primarily an overview, not expert configuration or troubleshooting content.
